text.title: java-spring Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for java-spring as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Controller returns entity type directly: `public User findById(Long id)` without DTO.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lazy-loading fields serialize to null. Hibernate proxies leak into JSON. Schema changes break API contracts. Always use DTOs.

failure-mode.description: Service method missing `@Transactional` on multi-step writes (save → update → delete sequence).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each JDBC call auto-commits. Partial failure leaves inconsistent state. Partial rollback impossible.

failure-mode.description: Calling another `@Transactional` method on `this` instead of injecting the bean.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Spring proxies don't wrap self-invocation. The called method runs without a transaction, silently.

failure-mode.description: Lazy-loading a collection in the controller and then serializing it: `return user.getRoles().stream()...`
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LazyInitializationException if the session is closed after the service call. Move the fetch into the repository query or service layer.

failure-mode.description: Endpoint returning `List<DTO>` from `findAll()` on a 10M-row table.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OOM, timeout, and query lock contention. Prod incident.

failure-mode.description: Loading a user, then looping over roles inside the controller and issuing a separate query per role.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: N+1 queries. 1 user + 100 roles = 101 queries. Explicit `LEFT JOIN FETCH` solves this in 1 query.

failure-mode.description: Service returning `null` or an empty Optional without throwing an exception.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Controller must null-check every result. Inconsistent error messages. Global advice cannot handle it uniformly.
